_meta.dna: appstore(lifecycle) = browse(catalog) x detail(view) x install(app) x run(recipe) x approve(action) x evidence(seal) x uninstall(clean)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor has 7 seed questions from a specific persona context. Questions must be answerable by checking if something actually works — click it, see it, verify it. Not theoretical.

name: Tower of App Store & Lifecycle
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT(7q) -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

floors.persona_context: 27, Seattle, UX researcher, Intermediate tech, Free tier, exploring what apps are available before committing to Solace
questions: Can I open the App Store page in Solace Browser and see a grid of available apps with icons, names, and one-line descriptions?
Does the App Store have categories (Email, Social, Productivity, Finance) that I can click to filter the app list?
Can I search for apps by typing 'gmail' in a search bar and see matching results instantly?
Does each app tile show whether it is free, requires a Starter plan, or requires Pro — before I click into it?
Can I sort apps by 'Most Popular', 'Newest', or 'Category' to find what I want quickly?
Does the App Store load and display all apps within 3 seconds on a normal broadband connection?
If there are no apps matching my search query, does the page show 'No results found' rather than a blank screen?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Olivia is evaluating Solace. The App Store is her first impression. It must look polished, load fast, and help her find relevant apps without confusion.

floors.persona_context: 55, Warsaw, Small business owner, Beginner tech, Starter tier, needs to understand what an app does before installing
questions: When I click on an app in the catalog, does a detail page open showing the full description, screenshots, and feature list?
Does the detail page show the app's required permissions (email access, browser automation) in plain language I can understand?
Can I see which tier is required to use this app (Free, Starter, Pro) and what happens if I am on a lower tier?
Does the detail page show a version number and last updated date so I know the app is maintained?
Is there a 'Back to Catalog' button that returns me to the App Store without losing my scroll position?
Does the detail page show the app's estimated cost per run (LLM tokens) so I can budget?
If the app requires a connected account (Google, LinkedIn), does the detail page say so before I install?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Tomasz is non-technical and cautious. The detail page must give him all the information needed to make an install decision without jargon or hidden requirements.

floors.persona_context: 32, Lagos, Digital marketer, Intermediate tech, Pro tier, installing multiple apps for email + social workflow
questions: Can I click 'Install' on an app and see it download and install within 5 seconds without leaving the page?
After installation, does the app immediately appear in my sidebar under 'Installed Apps' with a status badge?
If I try to install an app that requires Pro tier but I am on Starter, does it show an upgrade prompt instead of failing silently?
Can I install 5 apps in sequence without any of them conflicting or overwriting each other's configuration?
Does the install button change to 'Installed' with a checkmark after successful installation?
If installation fails due to a network error, does the app show a retry button with the specific error message?
Can I see a list of all my installed apps in one place with install date and version for each?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Zainab installs multiple apps at once. Install flow must be fast, reliable, handle errors gracefully, and provide clear status feedback.

floors.persona_context: 46, Gothenburg, Account manager at consulting firm, Beginner tech, Pro tier, running his first app recipe to automate client follow-ups
questions: After installing the Gmail Triage app, can I click 'Run' and see the app's recipe start executing with a progress indicator?
Does the run flow show me what the recipe is doing at each step (connecting to Gmail, scanning inbox, generating drafts)?
If the recipe requires my Google account but I have not connected one yet, does it prompt me to connect before running?
Can I cancel a running recipe mid-execution and see it stop cleanly without leaving partially-completed actions?
Does the run flow show estimated time remaining based on the number of emails or items being processed?
If the recipe encounters an error (API timeout, invalid credential), does it show the error in plain language with a suggested fix?
After the recipe completes, does it show a summary: 15 emails scanned, 8 actions suggested, 0 executed (awaiting approval)?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Anders is running his first recipe. The run flow must be transparent, show progress, handle errors gracefully, and never execute actions without his approval.

floors.persona_context: 40, Madrid, Legal operations manager, Expert tech, Enterprise tier, reviews every automated action before approval for compliance
questions: When a recipe suggests an action, does the approval modal show the complete action detail — not just 'Send email' but the full email content?
Does the approval modal clearly show the action type (send, archive, delete, forward) with a color-coded risk level (green/yellow/red)?
Can I approve low-risk actions (archive) in batch while reviewing high-risk actions (send, delete) individually?
Does the modal show which account and which recipient the action targets so I do not accidentally act on the wrong account?
If I reject an action, does the modal ask for a rejection reason that gets logged in the evidence chain?
Can I click 'View Original' in the approval modal to see the source email or document the action is based on?
Does the approval modal display correctly on a 1366x768 laptop screen without requiring horizontal scrolling?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Maria is in legal ops. The approval modal is the last safety gate before actions execute. It must show complete context, log rejections, and work on standard laptop screens.

floors.persona_context: 50, Johannesburg, Audit partner at mid-tier firm, Expert tech, Enterprise tier, needs tamper-evident records of every automated action
questions: After I approve and execute an action, does the app create an evidence record with SHA-256 hash, timestamp, user ID, and action payload?
Can I view the evidence chain for a specific app's executions in chronological order with hash links between records?
Does each evidence record include a link to the previous record's hash, forming an unbroken chain?
If I export the evidence chain as JSON, does it include all fields needed for an external audit tool to verify integrity?
Can I search evidence records by date range, action type, or target account to find specific actions quickly?
Does the evidence system store the full input (original email) and full output (sent reply) — not just metadata?
If someone tampers with an evidence record in local storage, does the verification tool detect the broken hash chain?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: David is an auditor. Evidence must be cryptographically verifiable, exportable, searchable, and include full content — not just metadata. Incomplete evidence chains are audit failures.

floors.persona_context: 36, Sydney, Operations coordinator, Intermediate tech, Starter tier, needs to uninstall apps she no longer uses and manage storage
questions: Can I go to my installed apps list and click 'Uninstall' on an app I no longer need?
Does uninstalling an app remove its configuration, cached data, and sidebar entry completely?
After uninstalling, does the app revert to 'Install' status in the App Store catalog so I can reinstall later if needed?
Does uninstalling an app preserve my evidence chain for actions I previously executed (evidence is immutable)?
Can I see how much local storage each installed app is using so I can decide which to uninstall?
If I try to uninstall an app that has pending approved-but-not-executed actions, does it warn me first?
Can I update an app to a newer version from the installed apps list without losing my configuration and connected accounts?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Emma needs clean app lifecycle management. Uninstall must be complete but preserve evidence. Updates must preserve config. Storage visibility helps her manage disk space.
